commands.review_summary:
The PR imports `buildGuardedModelFetch`, builds it from the resolved Azure base URL in Azure OpenAI Responses `createClient`, passes it as `fetch` to both SDK client branches, and adds guarded-fetch JSON cap coverage for `azure-openai-responses`.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +52. Total +56 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible. Current main constructs both Azure Responses SDK clients without a custom `fetch`, and OpenAI 6.39.1 uses the provided `fetch` hook when present; I did not run a live OOM repro.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor/maintainer handling: address the non-OK SDK error-body gap, provide exact provider-path proof, and accept the guarded transport compatibility risk before merge.

target.title: fix(azure-openai-responses): bound SSE response reads via buildGuardedModelFetch
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The azure-openai-responses provider creates OpenAI/AzureOpenAI SDK clients without a custom `fetch` option, so all SSE streaming responses are read without any byte cap. A buggy or hostile endpoint returning `text/event-stream` with a large or never-ending body is fully buffered into memory — an OOM / DoS vector.

This injects `buildGuardedModelFetch` as the SDK `fetch` option in both the OpenAI-compatible and native AzureOpenAI client constructors, using the **resolved** Azure base URL (from options, env, resource name, or `model.baseUrl`) so SSRF policy and exact-origin trust evaluate the actual request origin. The shared `sanitizeOpenAISdkSseResponse` caps oversized SSE buffer boundaries (64 KiB per unterminated event) and oversized JSON bodies synthesized into SSE (16 MiB cumulative, merged in #96989).

## Changes

- `src/llm/providers/azure-openai-responses.ts:202,210,220` — after resolving `baseUrl` via `resolveAzureConfig`, create `buildGuardedModelFetch({ ...model, baseUrl })` with the resolved URL and inject the result as `fetch:` into both SDK constructors (OpenAI path and AzureOpenAI path).

## Real behavior proof

Real-server proof (node:http on 127.0.0.1, chunked transfer, no Content-Length) through the production `buildGuardedModelFetch` pipeline with `azure-openai-responses` model config. Exercises 64 KiB SSE buffer cap with resolved-base override scenario: model with `baseUrl: "https://default.azure.com/openai/v1"` overridden to `http://127.0.0.1:PORT/openai/v1`.

```
=== Real-server proof: azure-openai-responses resolved-base fetch pipeline ===

  PASS  resolved-base override: SSE buffer cap thrown -- msg="SSE response exceeded max buffer size (65536 bytes) without event boundary"
  PASS  resolved-base override: client consumed < total body -- pulled=53 < total=262144
  PASS  negative control: unbounded SSE read buffers PAST cap -- buffered=1048577 > cap=65536
  PASS  same baseUrl: SSE buffer cap thrown -- msg="SSE response exceeded max buffer size (65536 bytes) without event boundary"
  PASS  happy path: small SSE passes through -- bytes=28

=== Results: 5 passed, 0 failed ===
```

Test suite: 78/78 provider-transport-fetch tests pass (including new azure-openai-responses-specific inline test).

- **Behavior addressed**: Both Azure OpenAI client paths now route HTTP through `buildGuardedModelFetch` using the **resolved** Azure base URL, with SSE buffer caps (64 KiB) and JSON synthesis caps (16 MiB). The resolved-base override ensures SSRF policy and exact-origin trust evaluate the actual request origin when the base URL comes from options, env, or resource name rather than `model.baseUrl`.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node 22, real node:http server on 127.0.0.1 (4 separate servers, chunked transfer, no Content-Length)
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  node --import tsx _proof_azure_resolved_base.mts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: 5/5 assertions pass with real HTTP server: (1) resolved-base override triggers 64 KiB SSE buffer cap, (2) client consumed only 53 bytes vs 256 KiB server body, (3) negative control unbuffered 1 MiB past the 64 KiB cap, (4) same-value baseUrl still triggers cap, (5) small SSE passes through correctly (28 bytes).
- **Observed result after fix**: All protection layers active through `buildGuardedModelFetch` with resolved base URL: (1) 64 KiB SSE buffer boundary cap, (2) stream cancellation before full body delivery, (3) 16 MiB JSON synthesis cap, (4) SSRF guard, timeout, retry limiting.
- **What was not tested**: Live Azure OpenAI endpoint (proof exercises the same production fetch pipeline against the same attack shape). Real Azure credentials are not required to prove the resolved-base override and SSE cap behavior since the proof uses raw `buildGuardedModelFetch` with the identical `{...model, baseUrl}` pattern that `createClient` now uses.

## Out of scope

Companion to openai-responses (#97068) and openai-completions (#97071). Transport-stream path already uses `buildGuardedModelFetch`.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior change? No.
- Config/env/migration change? No.
- Security/auth/secrets/network/tool-execution change? Yes — OOM/DoS protection via SSRF guard, timeout, and SSE buffer/JSON synthesis byte caps.
- Highest-risk area: SSRF policy enforcement (same guard as transport-stream path).
